evidence: I don't know if I would sign up for Elon's neural link, but I would definitely consider some form of biomodification if needed. Discussing smart glasses that provide heads-up information and integrate with Zo to create superpower of total knowledge or omniscience.
Personal AI systems are not separate tools but natural cognitive prosthetics that extend human capability. The boundary between internal and external cognition is blurring.

source_display: Technical Sophistication Paradox in Product Positioning
evidence: The folks I've seen shit on them on Twitter are actually really technical folks that are expecting Zo to be for them. But the problem is those folks, the folks that appreciate the value of the server go, well, I don't need Zo for this. And the folks that don't appreciate the value of having your personal server don't appreciate it and therefore aren't responsive to that messaging.

target_display: Technical Sophistication Paradox in Product Positioning
evidence: Value comprehension follows an inverse U-curve with technical capability. At low technical capability, users evaluate tools by outcomes. At high technical capability, users evaluate tools by components. Both groups miss the middle ground.

source_display: Sweet Spot ICP: Just Smart Enough
evidence: The real opportunity lies with the middle segment—people competent enough to use ChatGPT projects or custom GPTs but who hit concrete limitations when they try to do serious work. They're smart enough to feel the pain but not technical enough to architect their own infrastructure.

source_display: Sweet Spot ICP: Just Smart Enough

evidence: Careerspan has a careerspan-esque problem in that they don't know how to communicate the value of something that sounds sort of nebulous. The pattern holds across categories.

source_display: Internal Mobility as Second GTM Track
evidence: At that point sourcing and matching become the same thing. Two distinct audiences require different hooks but share the same underlying product demonstration. Internal Mobility: The best hire is who you already have.
